title: Pentisea gemmata
taxonomy: Pentisea gemmata is a species of lichenized fungus in the family Pyrenulaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of Ascomycota, a division of fungi characterized by the production of ascospores. Ascomycota is further divided into various classes, with Pyrenulaceae being a part of the class Dothideomycetes. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Pentisea gemmata relative to other fungi. The family Pyrenulaceae is notable for containing lichens that are often crustose or squamulose in growth form.
